title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: Read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.

content.text: The question of how human language evolved remains one of the most (0) enduring puzzles in science. Unlike tools or pottery, spoken words leave no physical trace, making it difficult for archaeologists to (1) a timeline. Most scholars agree that language probably (2) from simpler forms of communication used by our ancestors. These early signals might have (3) of basic vocalizations and gestures aimed at coordinating group activities. As social structures became more complex, the need for a more sophisticated system of communication (4) as well. This eventually led to the development of grammar, which allowed humans to convey abstract ideas and share information about the past or future. Some researchers (5) that the ability to speak was a sudden mutation, while others believe it was a gradual process spanning thousands of years. Regardless of its origin, language has (6) humans to collaborate on an unprecedented scale. It is the very (7) of our culture, allowing us to pass knowledge from one generation to the next. Without it, the rapid progress of our species would have been (8) impossible. Even today, linguists continue to study remote languages to better understand the underlying structures that unite all human speech, helping us learn about our shared heritage and evolution.

example.explanation: “Enduring puzzle” is a standard collocation for a long-lasting mystery.

questions.explanation: “Establish a timeline” is a common academic collocation meaning to determine the order of events.
“Emerged from” is frequently used to describe the evolutionary appearance of a new trait.
“Consisted of” is the correct phrasal verb here. “Composed” would require ‘was composed of’.
“A need grew” is a common way to describe an increasing requirement.
“Contend” in an academic sense means to argue or assert a position.
“Enabled” means to give the means or opportunity to do something.
“Backbone” is used figuratively to mean the most important part of something.
“Virtually impossible” is a fixed adverbial collocation meaning almost completely impossible.
